name: Mercury-Redstone 3
description: First American crewed spaceflight, a suborbital flight piloted by Alan Shepard.

name: Mercury-Atlas 6
description: First American crewed orbital flight, piloted by John Glenn for three orbits.

name: Gemini 4
description: First American spacewalk, performed by Ed White during a four-day flight.

name: Gemini 7
description: 14-day endurance flight that served as rendezvous target for Gemini 6A.

name: Gemini 8
description: First docking of two spacecraft in orbit, cut short by a stuck thruster.

name: Gemini 12
description: Final Gemini mission, demonstrating sustained EVA work by Buzz Aldrin.

name: Apollo 1
description: Pre-flight test ended in a launchpad cabin fire that killed all three crew members.

name: Apollo 7
description: First crewed Apollo flight, an 11-day Earth-orbital shakedown of the Command Module.

name: Apollo 8
description: First crewed mission to leave Earth orbit and orbit the Moon.

name: Apollo 9
description: Earth-orbit test of the Lunar Module rendezvous and docking procedures.

name: Apollo 10
description: Lunar-orbit dress rehearsal that descended to within 15 km of the Moon's surface.

name: Apollo 11
description: First crewed mission to land humans on the Moon, with Armstrong and Aldrin on the surface.

name: Apollo 12
description: Second crewed Moon landing, touching down near the Surveyor 3 robotic lander.

name: Apollo 13
description: Aborted lunar landing after an oxygen tank explosion; the crew returned safely using the LM as a lifeboat.

name: Apollo 14
description: Third crewed Moon landing, exploring the Fra Mauro highlands.

name: Apollo 15
description: First mission to use the Lunar Roving Vehicle, exploring Hadley-Apennine.

name: Apollo 16
description: Crewed landing in the Descartes Highlands with extensive geology traverses.

name: Apollo 17
description: Final and longest crewed Moon landing, including the only geologist astronaut.

name: Skylab 2
description: First crewed mission to Skylab; repaired thermal damage sustained during launch.

name: Skylab 4
description: 84-day record-setting Skylab mission and final visit to the station.

name: Apollo-Soyuz Test Project
description: First crewed international docking, between an Apollo capsule and a Soviet Soyuz.

name: Mariner 2
description: First spacecraft to fly past another planet, returning Venus atmospheric data.

name: Mariner 4
description: First successful Mars flyby, returning the first close-up images of another planet.

name: Mariner 9
description: First spacecraft to enter orbit around another planet, mapping Mars from above.

name: Mariner 10
description: First spacecraft to use a gravity assist and to visit Mercury, with three flybys.

name: Pioneer 10
description: First spacecraft to traverse the asteroid belt and to fly past Jupiter.

name: Pioneer 11
description: Second spacecraft to fly past Jupiter and the first to visit Saturn.

name: Viking 1
description: First successful Mars lander, returning images and biological experiment data.

name: Viking 2
description: Second Mars lander, paired with an orbiter studying the planet's surface.

name: Voyager 1
description: Outer-planets probe that reached interstellar space and remains operational.

name: Voyager 2
description: Only spacecraft to visit all four outer planets, now in interstellar space.

name: STS-1
description: First Space Shuttle flight, an orbital test of Columbia piloted by Young and Crippen.

name: STS-7
description: First American woman in space, Sally Ride, aboard Challenger.

name: STS-51-L
description: Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch, killing all seven crew members.

name: STS-31
description: Deployed the Hubble Space Telescope into low Earth orbit.

name: STS-61
description: First Hubble servicing mission, correcting the telescope's spherical aberration.

name: STS-71
description: First docking of a Space Shuttle with the Russian space station Mir.

name: STS-88
description: First Shuttle mission to assemble the International Space Station.

name: STS-107
description: Columbia broke apart on re-entry, killing all seven crew members.

name: STS-125
description: Final Hubble servicing mission, extending the telescope's lifetime.

name: STS-135
description: Final flight of the Space Shuttle program.

name: Magellan
description: Venus orbiter that radar-mapped 98 percent of the planet's surface.

name: Galileo
description: First spacecraft to orbit Jupiter and the first to drop a probe into its atmosphere.

name: Cassini-Huygens
description: Saturn orbiter that delivered the Huygens probe to the surface of Titan.

name: Juno
description: Jupiter polar orbiter studying the planet's gravity field, magnetosphere, and origin.

name: Mars Pathfinder
description: Mars lander that delivered the small Sojourner rover, the first wheels on Mars.

name: Mars Global Surveyor
description: Long-running Mars orbiter that mapped the planet at high resolution for nine years.

name: Mars Climate Orbiter
description: Lost on Mars arrival due to a unit-conversion error between metric and imperial.

name: Mars Polar Lander
description: Crashed during landing on Mars due to a premature engine shutdown.

name: 2001 Mars Odyssey
description: Mars orbiter still operational and the longest-running spacecraft at Mars.

name: Spirit
description: Mars Exploration Rover that operated for over six years before becoming stuck in soft soil.

name: Opportunity
description: Mars Exploration Rover that traveled over 45 km and lasted nearly 15 years on Mars.

name: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter
description: High-resolution Mars orbiter providing imaging and communications relay.

name: Phoenix
description: Mars polar lander that confirmed the presence of water ice in the regolith.

name: Curiosity
description: Car-sized Mars rover studying habitability in Gale Crater, still active.

name: MAVEN
description: Mars orbiter studying the upper atmosphere and the loss of the planet's air to space.

name: InSight
description: Mars stationary lander that studied seismic activity and the planet's interior.

name: Mars 2020 Perseverance
description: Mars rover collecting rock samples for future return and supporting the Ingenuity helicopter.

name: Clementine
description: Joint NASA-DoD lunar orbiter that produced the first global topographic map of the Moon.

name: Lunar Prospector
description: Polar lunar orbiter that detected hydrogen, suggesting water ice in permanently shadowed craters.

name: LCROSS
description: Lunar impactor that confirmed water in a permanently shadowed crater near the south pole.

name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
description: High-resolution lunar mapping orbiter, still active.

name: GRAIL
description: Twin lunar orbiters that produced a high-resolution gravity map of the Moon.

name: NEAR Shoemaker
description: First spacecraft to orbit and then land on an asteroid (Eros).

name: Stardust
description: Comet sample-return mission that captured dust from comet Wild 2.

name: Genesis
description: Solar wind sample-return mission whose capsule crashed on landing but yielded usable samples.

name: Deep Impact
description: Comet mission that fired a copper impactor into comet Tempel 1 to study its interior.

name: Dawn
description: Ion-propulsion mission that orbited the asteroid Vesta and the dwarf planet Ceres.

name: OSIRIS-REx
description: Asteroid sample-return mission that delivered material from Bennu to Earth in 2023.

name: Lucy
description: Multi-target mission to visit several Jupiter Trojan asteroids.

name: DART
description: Kinetic-impactor planetary-defense test that altered the orbit of asteroid Dimorphos.

name: New Horizons
description: First spacecraft to fly past Pluto and the Kuiper Belt object Arrokoth.

name: SOHO
description: Joint NASA-ESA solar observatory at the L1 Lagrange point.

name: STEREO
description: Twin spacecraft providing stereoscopic views of solar activity.

name: Solar Dynamics Observatory
description: Geostationary observatory imaging the Sun in multiple wavelengths.

name: Parker Solar Probe
description: Closest-ever solar approach, designed to fly through the Sun's corona.

name: Hubble Space Telescope
description: Long-running optical space telescope launched by Shuttle and serviced five times.

name: Compton Gamma Ray Observatory
description: Gamma-ray space telescope that operated for nine years before controlled deorbit.

name: Chandra X-ray Observatory
description: X-ray space telescope in a highly elliptical Earth orbit.

name: Spitzer Space Telescope
description: Infrared space telescope in an Earth-trailing solar orbit.

name: Kepler
description: Exoplanet-hunting space telescope that monitored the same field for transits.

name: TESS
description: Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite scanning the whole sky for nearby exoplanets.

name: James Webb Space Telescope
description: Large infrared observatory at the Sun-Earth L2 Lagrange point.

name: Terra
description: Flagship Earth-observing satellite carrying five remote-sensing instruments.

name: Aqua
description: Earth-observing satellite focused on the planet's water cycle.

name: Aura
description: Earth-observing satellite measuring atmospheric chemistry and ozone.

name: OCO-2
description: Orbiting Carbon Observatory measuring atmospheric carbon dioxide.

name: ICESat-2
description: Earth-orbiting altimeter measuring ice-sheet mass changes with a laser.

name: Landsat 9
description: Land-imaging satellite continuing the longest-running Earth-observation program.

name: Expedition 1
description: First long-duration crew aboard the International Space Station.

name: SpaceX Demo-2
description: First crewed flight of the Crew Dragon and first US commercial-crew mission to the ISS.

name: Crew-1
description: First operational SpaceX Crew Dragon rotation mission to the ISS.

name: Boeing CFT
description: First crewed flight test of Boeing's Starliner capsule to the ISS.

name: Artemis I
description: Uncrewed test flight of the Space Launch System rocket and Orion capsule around the Moon.

name: Pioneer Venus 1
description: Venus orbiter that radar-mapped the planet at low resolution.

name: Pioneer Venus 2
description: Venus multiprobe mission that delivered four atmospheric probes.

name: Ulysses
description: Joint NASA-ESA mission that flew over the Sun's poles via a Jupiter gravity assist.

name: MESSENGER
description: First spacecraft to orbit Mercury, mapping its surface and composition.

name: Mars Observer
description: Mars orbiter lost just before orbit insertion due to a propulsion failure.

name: CONTOUR
description: Comet flyby mission lost shortly after a solid-rocket-motor burn.
